headlines.title: Asia Stocks Tumble On Tech Worries, Mideast War
Stock market today: Japan's Nikkei falls, South Korea's KOSPI plunges over 6 percent ahead of TSMC results as Wall Street futures rise
Asian shares mostly decline with South Korea's Kospi down 6.6%, while oil prices slip
Japan’s weak yen masks a structural crisis
As the AI rally loses steam, how resilient are Japanese stocks?
Japan’s Nikkei Shaves 3% This Morning As Chip Stocks Sink
U.S. stocks rise within 0.5% of their record, even as oil prices keep climbing

marketTone.ko: 오늘 아시아 증시는 기술주 부진과 중동 지정학적 우려로 인해 전반적으로 크게 하락했습니다. 특히 TSMC 실적 발표를 앞두고 AI 랠리 동력 상실 우려가 커지면서 일본 닛케이 지수는 3%, 한국 코스피는 6% 이상 급락했습니다. 한편, 일본의 엔화 약세가 구조적 위기를 가리고 있다는 지적이 제기되며 일본 증시의 회복 탄력성에 대한 의문이 커지고 있습니다.
marketTone.en: Asian stock markets saw significant declines today, primarily driven by concerns over the tech sector, with Japan's Nikkei falling 3% and South Korea's KOSPI plunging over 6% amidst worries about the AI rally losing steam and ahead of TSMC results. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East also contributed to the cautious sentiment. Furthermore, Japan's weak yen was highlighted as potentially masking deeper structural issues, raising questions about the resilience of Japanese stocks.
